If this is what I think it is, staying isn't the brave choice. It's the stupid one
I'd keep my distance. Whatever this is, it isn't getting smaller
Nobody's been hurt yet. I keep telling myself that

Don't go poking around after dark. I mean it. Not anymore
There's a line between caution and cowardice, and I know which side I'm on
Whatever you do, don't go alone. That's not wisdom, that's arithmetic
I've seen what happens to the overconfident. I'd rather be called a coward
It's getting worse. Slowly, but the direction is clear enough
The real danger isn't the thing itself. It's how careless people get when nothing happens for a while
I give it a week before someone does something reckless. Maybe less
If you're passing through, pass through quickly. That's my honest advice
We're not equipped for this. I don't think anyone is, but especially not us
The signs are all there if you're willing to read them. Most people aren't
Best case, it blows over on its own. I'm not counting on best case
I've survived enough to know when something doesn't feel right. This doesn't feel right
Caution is free. Recklessness costs everything
We haven't lost anyone yet. I intend to keep it that way
There's a window where you can still leave safely. I don't know how long it stays open
I'm not going to tell you what to do. But I'll tell you what I wouldn't do: I wouldn't go out there
The people who say there's no danger are the same people who never check
Every day nothing happens is a day closer to the day something does
I've seen settlements ignore warnings before. Those settlements aren't around to regret it
It's the kind of risk where being cautious looks foolish right up until it saves your life
I trust my gut, and my gut says this is the part where you start paying attention
There's a false sense of safety in numbers. Numbers don't help if you're not prepared
I'd rather plan for the worst and be pleasantly surprised than the opposite
The moment you stop treating it seriously is the moment it becomes serious
I know people who've walked toward danger out of curiosity. I also know their graves
Whatever is happening, it doesn't care about our opinions on it
I've told the children to stay close. That should tell you where my head is
The danger isn't immediate, but it's patient. Patient things outlast hasty ones
If half of what I've heard is true, caution is an understatement
People keep saying 'it can't get worse.' It absolutely can. And it has, before
There's comfort in pretending everything's fine. Comfort and nothing else
I've started thinking in terms of escape routes. That's new for me

The risk isn't a cliff you fall off. It's a slope you don't notice until you're sliding
A healthy fear is the only thing that keeps unhealthy outcomes at bay
I'd rather be the one who worried for nothing than the one who didn't worry enough
Anyone who says they're not concerned either knows something I don't or doesn't know enough
It's the quiet dangers that get you. The loud ones at least give you warning
I've checked the locks twice tonight. I usually don't check them at all
Preparedness isn't paranoia. Paranoia is what happens when you don't prepare
The worst thing about this kind of danger is that it doesn't announce itself clearly
I'm keeping my options open. By which I mean I know three ways out of this settlement

I wouldn't send my worst enemy out there right now. And I have a good imagination for enemies
The bravest people I know are also the most careful. There's a lesson in that

Some risks you can calculate. This one has too many unknowns for comfort

I've been wrong about threats before: usually by underestimating them

I keep a weapon within reach now. Not because I expect to use it. Because I can't afford not to
We're safe for now. 'For now' is doing a lot of work in that sentence

I'm not trying to scare you. I'm trying to make sure you're paying attention

There's a difference between unlikely and impossible. I've stopped confusing the two
If someone tells you there's nothing to worry about, ask them what they've done to check
The fact that we're still here doesn't mean we're safe. It means we've been lucky
I keep telling people to stay alert. Most of them nod and go right back to what they were doing

It would take one bad night to change everything. Just one
I measure safety in hours now. Used to measure it in seasons

If you're the type to laugh off warnings, I'd suggest adjusting that habit
This isn't the kind of threat you outrun. It's the kind you outlast. And that requires thought
The most dangerous moment is right after you think the danger has passed
Keep your back to something solid and your eyes forward. Old advice, but it's old for a reason
I'm past the point of hoping it'll resolve itself. I'm at the point of asking what we do if it doesn't

I've known people who walked into danger thinking they were walking past it. The difference matters
There's a version of this where nobody gets hurt. It requires everyone to take it seriously
I'd give it even odds right now. Those aren't the odds I'd choose to live with

I used to think caution was a sign of weakness. I know better now

I'd rather lose a day to caution than lose a life to haste

The moment I stop worrying is the moment I know I've gotten too comfortable

I'm not alarmist. I just believe in acknowledging what's in front of me

This is the kind of situation where 'nothing happened today' is genuinely good news

When I catch myself relaxing, I remind myself why I shouldn't be

I'd rather overreact ten times than underreact once
Everything about this tells me to be careful. I intend to listen
